title: True research lane: Architecture exploration lane
hypothesis: Try a structural model or algorithm change distinct from scalar tuning.

title: True research lane: Optimizer/training recipe lane
hypothesis: Try a materially different optimizer, scheduler, or training recipe.

title: True research lane: Data/curriculum lane
hypothesis: Try data ordering, sampling, or curriculum changes.

title: Foundation milestone: Create TypeScript project scaffold with Vitest
hypothesis: A minimal TS/Vitest scaffold gives all lanes a safe place to add deterministic chess and runtime tests, increasing implementation velocity without touching benchmark semantics. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Implement board state and legal move generation
hypothesis: Correct chess rules are the foundation for policy masking and PUCT; perft-style tests will prevent silent engine regressions. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Freeze fixed move indexing and golden fixtures
hypothesis: A stable legal-move codec prevents the highest-risk failure mode identified in the paper: training/export/inference encoding mismatch. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Implement lc0-like feature encoder
hypothesis: A deterministic board-to-plane encoder enables both neural training and browser inference while keeping the first model classic-conv friendly. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Build PUCT search around abstract evaluator
hypothesis: Separating PUCT from neural backend lets us test search with mock policy/WDL outputs before real ONNX models exist. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Define tiny conv student model spec
hypothesis: A 16x2/24x3 micro model and 48x5/64x6 balanced model spec provides concrete training/export targets aligned with the research paper. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Create ONNX Runtime Web evaluator interface
hypothesis: A backend-neutral evaluator interface with ORT Web implementation will support WASM baseline and WebGPU acceleration without coupling engine logic to runtime details. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Design teacher-label data format and distillation loss
hypothesis: A documented data schema for positions, legal masks, search policy, and WDL/Q targets unlocks offline training without AlphaZero-scale self-play. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: Foundation milestone: Add evaluation gauntlet skeleton
hypothesis: Tracking depth-0 move quality, fixed-playout matches, latency, and model size early will make future improvements comparable and prevent benchmark drift. This is shared pre-work, not a differentiated research lane.

title: True research lane: PUCT search policy experiments
hypothesis: Once the substrate is stable, compare structural PUCT choices such as FPU, policy temperature, root noise, and uncertainty-based playout allocation against fixed evaluator fixtures.

title: True research lane: browser inference backends
hypothesis: Compare ORT Web WASM/SIMD, WebGPU/FP16, worker batching, and model caching for latency and compatibility without changing chess semantics.

title: True research lane: tiny student architectures
hypothesis: Compare 16x2/24x3/48x5/64x6 conv students under the same move encoding and feature fixtures to find best strength-per-byte.

title: True research lane: distillation target choices
hypothesis: Compare raw teacher policy, search-visit policy, WDL/Q mixes, and calibration losses while keeping model architecture and engine runtime fixed.

title: True research lane: evaluation and product fitness
hypothesis: Build gauntlets that separately measure policy quality, fixed-playout strength, latency, model size, and browser compatibility to replace the bootstrap score later.

title: True research lane: board-square student representation
hypothesis: A student using explicit 64-square piece/color features should beat the current aggregate linear FEN baseline on fixed teacher_distill_seed_v2 policy/WDL metrics without changing labels.

title: True research lane: small MLP student
hypothesis: A dependency-free small hidden-layer student should learn non-linear board patterns and improve distillation score over linear features on teacher_distill_seed_v2.

title: True research lane: teacher-source ablations
hypothesis: Training on lc0-only, Stockfish-only, and combined teacher labels will identify whether the two teachers help or conflict on the fixed v2 seed dataset.

title: True research lane: policy vs WDL loss mixes
hypothesis: Separating policy-only, WDL-only, and combined losses can improve the primary distillation score or reveal objective conflict on the fixed v2 teacher labels.

title: Compression lane: quantized JSON serialization for 64x6 conv student
hypothesis: Serializing trained policy/WDL weights with deterministic decimal/float16-style quantization or shared tables can reduce model_json_bytes substantially while preserving distill_student_score within tolerance and browser JSON compatibility.

source: user request to play against the engine
title: Foundation: playable engine integration benchmark scaffold
hypothesis: Before playable-engine work becomes a research lane, build a comparable gauntlet that loads the promoted 64x6 student into legal move selection/PUCT and emits fixed metrics such as legal_move_rate, nodes_per_second, fixed-position move quality, and browser compatibility.

title: Policy-preserving WDL repair lane for 64x6 conv student
hypothesis: Because the 64x6 gain comes from better policy CE while WDL CE worsens, a structural value-head repair such as separate compact WDL features, calibrated Q auxiliary diagnostics, or value-head-only postfit may improve WDL without degrading policy CE or primary score.

title: Self-play teacher mix ratio sweep
hypothesis: A small amount of PUCT self-play visit-policy data mixed with teacher distillation can improve arena score over the current 64x6 baseline while preserving legality and avoiding self-play collapse.

title: Self-play visit budget and temperature sweep
hypothesis: Changing self-play visits and policy temperature may produce less overfit or more diverse training targets, improving arena score at the same teacher/self-play mix.

title: Larger mirrored opening-start arena discrimination
hypothesis: Using more arena games with fixed mirrored opening starts will reduce noise and reveal whether positive self-play mix weights improve actual play versus the weight=0 teacher baseline.

title: Cached baseline/control artifact for mix sweeps
hypothesis: Skipping repeated weight=0 retraining and treating teacher-only as a cached control will reduce sweep variance and wall-clock while making positive self-play weights the primary comparison target.

title: Rust WASM in-browser evaluator/search path
hypothesis: Compiling the Rust evaluator/search core to WASM and calling it directly from the Chessground UI will remove the Node native-binary dependency and advance the true in-browser Leela-like engine milestone without sacrificing Rust correctness.

title: Self-play opening diversity and early-ply noise
hypothesis: Adding fixed opening starts and early-ply policy temperature/noise to Rust self-play will produce more diverse visit-policy targets and reduce overfitting to short homogeneous openings.

title: Scaled true-play arena games before strength claims
hypothesis: Increasing arena games to 32-128 with terminal/true-play scoring will reduce variance and prevent value-adjudication artifacts from being mistaken for real strength gains.

title: Scaled diverse self-play replay buffer
hypothesis: Generating 64-512 Rust self-play games with longer plies, opening starts, and early-ply stochasticity will produce a replay buffer diverse enough for self-play mix weights to improve true-play arena results.

title: Batched GPU neural evaluation for many self-play games
hypothesis: Running many Rust PUCT games in flight while batching leaf policy/WDL evaluations through tinygrad CUDA or ONNX will make larger nets and higher visit counts practical, even though tree management remains CPU-bound.

title: Larger capacity student trained on scaled self-play
hypothesis: Once data volume is increased, a larger learned/tensor-trained policy-WDL student can use the extra self-play examples better than the current frozen-feature linear heads, improving true-play arena score.

title: Teacher-assisted self-play correction buffer
hypothesis: Adding Stockfish/lc0 correction labels for repetition, max-ply, low-confidence, and no-progress self-play positions will produce more purposeful policy targets than raw weak self-play, improving readiness metrics before true-play arena scaling.
